zh.Newton St Loe, Somerset, England: 英國薩默塞特牛頓 聖 勒
zh.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo是Sir Roger de Saint Lo的兒子，於1212年出生在英國薩默塞特牛頓 聖 勒。他至少有1個兒子。他去世於1263年享年51歲。

zh-hans.Newton St Loe, Somerset, England: 英国薩默塞特牛頓 聖 勒

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Lo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Chao, Kam, Chun, Ming, Wei, Wing, Kin, Kwok, Wan, Ying, Chi.  Korean Chung, Pheng, Chong, Pao, Chang, Cho, Pak, Shoua, Thai, Toua, Yer, Blia.  Vietnamese Hieu, Hung, Phuc, Thong, Tien, Vinh, Bao, Bien, Chanh, Chau, Coong, Dinh Van.
Chinese: Cantonese form and alternative Mandarin form of the surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Chinese: alternative Mandarin form of the surname 駱, see   Luo   2.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo was born in 1212, in Newton St Loe, Somerset, England as the son of Sir Roger de Saint Lo. He had at least 1 son. He died in 1263, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo est né en 1212, à Newton St Loe, Somerset, Angleterre, fils de Sir Roger de Saint Lo. Il a eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1263, à l’âge de 51 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo wurde 1212 in Newton St Loe, Somerset, England, als Sohn von Sir Roger de Saint Lo geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1263 im Alter von 51 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo è nato il nel 1212 nel Newton St Loe, Somerset, England come figlio di Sir Roger de Saint Lo. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1263, all'età di 51 anni.

ja.Newton St Loe, Somerset, England: イギリスソメルセトニュートン スト ロ
ja.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Loは1212年にイギリスソメルセトニュートン スト ロでSir Roger de Saint Loの息子として生まれた。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1263年に51歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo(은)는 1212년 영국 서머세트 뉴턴 스트 로이에서 Sir Roger de Saint Lo의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1263년 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo nasceu em 1212, em Newton St Loe, Somerset, Inglaterra filho de Sir Roger de Saint Lo. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1263, com 51 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo родился в 1212 г. в местности нютон ст ло, сомирсет, Англия. Его отца звали Sir Roger de Saint Lo. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1263 г. в возрасте 51 год.

es.lifeSummary: Sir John de Saint Lo nació en 1212, en Newton St Loe, Somerset, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Roger de Saint Lo. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1263, a la edad de 51 años.
